CT.instructions:
Basic DSPy module that maps inputs to outputs using a language model.

Args:
    signature: The input/output signature describing the task.
    callbacks: Optional list of callbacks for instrumentation.
    **config: Default keyword arguments forwarded to the underlying
        language model. These values can be overridden for a single
        invocation by passing a ``config`` dictionary when calling the
        module. For example::

            predict = dspy.Predict("q -> a", rollout_id=1, temperature=1.0)
            predict(q="What is 1 + 52?", config={"rollout_id": 2, "temperature": 1.0})

str(object='') -> str
str(bytes_or_buffer[, encoding[, errors]]) -> str

Create a new string object from the given object. If encoding or
errors is specified, then the object must expose a data buffer
that will be decoded using the given encoding and error handler.
Otherwise, returns the result of object.__str__() (if defined)
or repr(object).
encoding defaults to 'utf-8'.
errors defaults to 'strict'.

case_id, modality, allowed_labels, report, ct_labels, cta_labels, ctp_labels -> labels

few_shot_examples.report_excerpt: EXAMINATION: CT STROKE BRAIN WO CONTRAST CLINICAL HISTORY: Neuro deficit acute stroke suspected COMPARISON: None. TECHNIQUE: Noncontrast head CT performed using radiation dose reduction techniques. Technical factors are evaluated and adjusted to ensure appropriate moderation of exposure. Automated dose management technology is applied to adjust radiation exposure while achieving a diagnostic quality image. FINDINGS: There is evidence of acute right MCA territorial infarction with loss of cortical gray-white matter differentiation of the mid lateral right precentral gyrus (series 303, image...

EXAMINATION: CT STROKE BRAIN WO CONTRAST COMPARISON: August 21, 2020. CLINICAL HISTORY: Neuro deficit acute stroke suspected COMMENTS: Axial noncontrast CT scan slices of the head were obtained. Sagittal and coronal reconstructions were obtained. CT imaging was performed with iterative reconstruction technique and/or automated exposure control to reduce radiation dose. FINDINGS: There is subtle cortical and subcortical low density at the left parietal lobe which is a progressive finding compared to the previous study. There is mild mucosal thickening in the visualized sinuses. There is calc...

EXAMINATION: CT STROKE BRAIN WO CONTRAST CLINICAL HISTORY: Neuro deficit acute stroke suspected COMPARISON: None. All CT images were acquired using low-dose technique with automated exposure control. IMPRESSION: Large occlusive hypodense clot in the M1 and M2 segment of the left MCA with acute infarction in the left basal ganglia and left corona radiata with is elevation of the cortical gray matter and an ASPECT score of approximately 8. There is no acute hemorrhage or mass effect. There is no midline shift. The ventricles are normal in size. The basal cisterns are patent. The calvarium is...

EXAMINATION: CT STROKE BRAIN WO CONTRAST CLINICAL HISTORY: Neuro deficit acute stroke suspected COMPARISON: None TECHNIQUE: Noncontrast CT of the brain was performed. Both soft tissue and bone reconstruction algorithms are interpreted. CT imaging was performed with iterative reconstruction techniques and/or automated exposure control to reduce radiation dose. FINDINGS: There is an acute infarct involving the entire left MCA and ACA territories (Aspects 0). There is mild local mass effect with some narrowing of the sulci. There is no midline shift. No hemorrhage is seen. The left carotid ter...

few_shot_examples.report_excerpt: EXAMINATION: CT BRAIN PERFUSION W RECON CLINICAL HISTORY: LVO COMPARISON: None TECHNIQUE: Whole brain CT perfusion was performed after administration of IV contrast. Post processing was performed. Dose lowering technique(s) such as automated exposure control, iterative reconstruction, and mA and/or KV adjustment for patient size was utilized for this examination. CT perfusion data was processed using automated software to assist stroke clinical decision-making. FINDINGS: Hypoperfusion volume (Tmax > 6 s, includes core and penumbra): 0 mL Infarct core volume (CBF <30% of contralateral): 0 mL...

EXAMINATION: CT BRAIN PERFUSION W RECON CLINICAL HISTORY: ams weakness COMPARISON: None TECHNIQUE: Whole brain CT perfusion was performed after administration of IV contrast. Post processing was performed. Dose lowering technique(s) such as automated exposure control, iterative reconstruction, and mA and/or KV adjustment for patient size was utilized for this examination. FINDINGS: No areas of abnormal hypoperfusion identified. Most recent CT head and CTA head demonstrate no evidence of cytotoxic edema or large vessel occlusion. Hypoperfusion volume (Tmax > 6 s, includes core and penumbra):...

EXAMINATION: CT BRAIN PERFUSION W RECON CLINICAL HISTORY: left m1 occlusion COMPARISON: None TECHNIQUE: Whole brain CT perfusion was performed after administration of IV contrast. Post processing was performed. Dose lowering technique(s) such as automated exposure control, iterative reconstruction, and mA and/or KV adjustment for patient size was utilized for this examination. FINDINGS: The CTA of the head demonstrates large occlusive thrombus in the M1 and M2 segment of the left MCA with collateral cortical leptomeningeal circulation. The CT of the head demonstrates acute ischemic changes...

EXAMINATION: CT BRAIN PERFUSION W RECON COMPARISON: CT scan of the head obtained at 6:25 PM. CLINICAL HISTORY: stroke COMMENTS: Axial postcontrast CT scan slices of the head were obtained. Postprocessing for angiographic evaluation including 3-D reconstructions was obtained. FINDINGS: Left-sided cord infarct by CBF less than 30% is 332 mL. Left-sided hypoperfusion by Tmax greater than 6 seconds is 343 mm. Mismatch volume is 11 mm. mismatch ratio is 1. IMPRESSION: Left-sided infarct and hypoperfusion as described above. 1RM1RAD_PS02
